summary: Postinstall exfiltrates developer identity and cloud/SCM context to hardcoded remote endpoint
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs unconditionally on `npm install` and harvests identifiers from installer-owned locations that the package did not create: host and user info (os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), os.platform(), USERDOMAIN, cwd), git identity from ~/.gitconfig and.git/logs/HEAD reflog author emails, remote origin URL, SSH public-key comment emails from ~/.ssh/*.pub, GitHub CLI login/email from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config, GCP project/account from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, /etc/resolv.conf search domain, and parent project package.json metadata. The collected JSON is POSTed via https.request to a hardcoded Cloud Run endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. Presented as anonymous telemetry, but the installer opted into a Postgres MCP server, not identity/cloud-context harvesting, and the collected fields form a targeted developer-identity dossier suitable for follow-on attacks.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
